title: Earliest Time to Finish One Task
content:
You are given a 2D integer array tasks where tasks[i] = [si, ti].

Each [si, ti] in tasks represents a task with start time si that takes ti units of time to finish.

Return the earliest time at which at least one task is finished.

Example 1:

Input: tasks = [[1,6],[2,3]]

Output: 5

Explanation: The first task starts at time t = 1 and finishes at time 1 + 6 = 7. The second task finishes at time 2 + 3 = 5. You can finish one task at time 5.

Example 2:

Input: tasks = [[100,100],[100,100],[100,100]]

Output: 200

Explanation: All three tasks finish at time 100 + 100 = 200.

Constraints:

• 1 <= tasks.length <= 100

• tasks[i] = [si, ti]

• 1 <= si, ti <= 100
hints: Compute finish[i] = s[i] + t[i] for each task and take the minimum.

solutions.explanation: Each task finishes at start + duration; return the minimum finish time across all tasks. O(n) time.
